trans-unit.source: QGIS Trigger Workflow for Somalia
trans-unit.target: QGIS Trigger Workflow for Somalia

trans-unit.source: Due to the changes in the availability of FEWSNET data, we are using IPC food insecurity classification instead of the FEWSNET classification. We are currently updating the documentation for the updated trigger model.
trans-unit.target: Due to the changes in the availability of FEWSNET data, we are using IPC food insecurity classification instead of the FEWSNET classification. We are currently updating the documentation for the updated trigger model.

trans-unit.source: The QGIS workflow presented in this article was developed in the framework of the Forecast-based-Action (FbF) Project of the Somalia Red Cresent Society (SRCS), the German Red Cross (GRC) and the Heidelberg Institute for Geoinformation Technology (HeiGIT).
trans-unit.target: The QGIS workflow presented in this article was developed in the framework of the Forecast-based-Action (FbF) Project of the Somalia Red Cresent Society (SRCS), the German Red Cross (GRC) and the Heidelberg Institute for Geoinformation Technology (HeiGIT).

trans-unit.source: The workflow consists of 15 steps of which 9 are automated in the form of a QGIS Model. In this article, we explain how all 15 steps can be done manually. In practice one needs to do only 6 steps manually, the rest is done by the QGSI Model. The chapter Workflow Automated explains the process and how it is intended in practice. The chapter Workflow Manually is to understand what happens inside of the QGIS Model.
trans-unit.target: The workflow consists of 15 steps of which 9 are automated in the form of a QGIS Model. In this article, we explain how all 15 steps can be done manually. In practice one needs to do only 6 steps manually, the rest is done by the QGSI Model. The chapter Workflow Automated explains the process and how it is intended in practice. The chapter Workflow Manually is to understand what happens inside of the QGIS Model.

trans-unit.source: Background
trans-unit.target: Background

trans-unit.source: Setting triggers is one of the cornerstones of the Forecast-based Financing system. For a National Society to have access to automatically released funding for their early actions, their Early Action Protocol needs to clearly define where and when funds will be allocated, and assistance will be provided. In FbF, this is decided according to specific threshold values, so-called triggers, based on weather and climate forecasts, which are defined for each region (see [FbF Manual](https://manual.forecast-based-financing.org/en/chapter/set-the-trigger/)).
trans-unit.target: Setting triggers is one of the cornerstones of the Forecast-based Financing system. For a National Society to have access to automatically released funding for their early actions, their Early Action Protocol needs to clearly define where and when funds will be allocated, and assistance will be provided. In FbF, this is decided according to specific threshold values, so-called triggers, based on weather and climate forecasts, which are defined for each region (see [FbF Manual](https://manual.forecast-based-financing.org/en/chapter/set-the-trigger/)).

trans-unit.source: For the development of the Somaliland-Somalia Drought Trigger mechanism various datasources were thoroughly analysed. Finally, the main parameters chosen for the trigger based on the historical impact assessment are the twelve month Standard Precipitation Index (SPI12) and the IPC acute food insecurity classification. The exact data used are the documented and forecasted SPI12 (source: ICPAC) and the forecasted IPC classification (8 month forecast, source: FEWSNET), that is used to calculate a population weighted index of food insecurity. The trigger thresholds for both components were optimised towards the most favourable proportion of hit rate and false alarm rate. The emerging thresholds were <-1 for the SPI12 and >=0,7 for the IPC based index. The triggering is done on district level and per district just one trigger initiation per year is possible.
trans-unit.target: For the development of the Somaliland-Somalia Drought Trigger mechanism various datasources were thoroughly analysed. Finally, the main parameters chosen for the trigger based on the historical impact assessment are the twelve month Standard Precipitation Index (SPI12) and the IPC acute food insecurity classification. The exact data used are the documented and forecasted SPI12 (source: ICPAC) and the forecasted IPC classification (8 month forecast, source: FEWSNET), that is used to calculate a population weighted index of food insecurity. The trigger thresholds for both components were optimised towards the most favourable proportion of hit rate and false alarm rate. The emerging thresholds were <-1 for the SPI12 and >=0,7 for the IPC based index. The triggering is done on district level and per district just one trigger initiation per year is possible.

trans-unit.source: Trigger Statement
trans-unit.target: Trigger Statement

trans-unit.source: When ICPAC issues a SPI-12 forecast of less than -1 for a district AND the current IPC food insecurity projection reaches at least 0.7 in its derived population weighted index in the same district, then we will act in this district. We expect the lead-time to be 90 days.
trans-unit.target: When ICPAC issues a SPI-12 forecast of less than -1 for a district AND the current IPC food insecurity projection reaches at least 0.7 in its derived population weighted index in the same district, then we will act in this district. We expect the lead-time to be 90 days.

trans-unit.source: Trigger Input Data
trans-unit.target: Trigger Input Data

trans-unit.source: For the trigger mechanism to work properly we currently use different datasets: data that we assume to be fixed in the near term, and variable data which describe the datasets that will be checked for triggering on a monthly base.
trans-unit.target: For the trigger mechanism to work properly we currently use different datasets: data that we assume to be fixed in the near term, and variable data which describe the datasets that will be checked for triggering on a monthly base.

trans-unit.source: Fixed Data
trans-unit.target: Fixed Data

trans-unit.source: By fixed data we mean datasets that are needed for the trigger to work, that will most probably not change in the near term. In the long term these datasets can be adapted easily.
trans-unit.target: By fixed data we mean datasets that are needed for the trigger to work, that will most probably not change in the near term. In the long term these datasets can be adapted easily.

trans-unit.source: Monitoring Data
trans-unit.target: Monitoring Data

trans-unit.source: The dataset used for monitoring the food insecurity phase has been updated to the classification and forecast published by the [Integrated Food Security Phase Classification](https://www.ipcinfo.org) as of March 2025. Prior to this, the food insecurity forecast published by FEWSNET had been used.
trans-unit.target: The dataset used for monitoring the food insecurity phase has been updated to the classification and forecast published by the [Integrated Food Security Phase Classification](https://www.ipcinfo.org) as of March 2025. Prior to this, the food insecurity forecast published by FEWSNET had been used.

trans-unit.source: The drought trigger mechanism is based on two variable monitoring datasets updated monthly: The SPI-12 forecast produced by ICPAC and the Food Insecurity projection produced by FEWSNET. The SPI-12 is used to capture hazard forecasts while the Food Insecurity Projection captures the dynamic vulnerability. In this way upcoming drought events (SPI) that most probably will lead to food insecurity (IPC) will be captured.
trans-unit.target: The drought trigger mechanism is based on two variable monitoring datasets updated monthly: The SPI-12 forecast produced by ICPAC and the Food Insecurity projection produced by FEWSNET. The SPI-12 is used to capture hazard forecasts while the Food Insecurity Projection captures the dynamic vulnerability. In this way upcoming drought events (SPI) that most probably will lead to food insecurity (IPC) will be captured.

trans-unit.source: five-phase scale providing common standards for classifying the severity of acute or anticipated acute food insecurity.
trans-unit.target: five-phase scale providing common standards for classifying the severity of acute or anticipated acute food insecurity.

trans-unit.source: What is the Standarized Precipitation Index (SPI-12)?
trans-unit.target: What is the Standarized Precipitation Index (SPI-12)?

trans-unit.source: The Standardized Precipitation Index (SPI) is a widely used index to characterize meteorological drought. The Standardized Precipitation Index (SPI-12) compares the total rainfall received at a particular location during the last 12 months with the long-term rainfall mean (42 years) for the same period of time at that location.
trans-unit.target: The Standardized Precipitation Index (SPI) is a widely used index to characterize meteorological drought. The Standardized Precipitation Index (SPI-12) compares the total rainfall received at a particular location during the last 12 months with the long-term rainfall mean (42 years) for the same period of time at that location.

trans-unit.source: What is IPC Food Security Projection Data?
trans-unit.target: What is IPC Food Security Projection Data?

trans-unit.source: The IPC is a commonly accepted measure and classification to describe the current and anticipated severity of acute food insecurity. The classification is based on a convergence of available data and evidence, including indicators related to food consumption, livelihoods, malnutrition and mortality. Food Insecurity is one of the prioritized impacts of droughts in Somalia which is why it is also used for the triggering mechanism, in a population-weighted index.
trans-unit.target: The IPC is a commonly accepted measure and classification to describe the current and anticipated severity of acute food insecurity. The classification is based on a convergence of available data and evidence, including indicators related to food consumption, livelihoods, malnutrition and mortality. Food Insecurity is one of the prioritized impacts of droughts in Somalia which is why it is also used for the triggering mechanism, in a population-weighted index.

trans-unit.source: Colour
trans-unit.target: Colour

trans-unit.source: Phase
trans-unit.target: Phase

trans-unit.source: Descriptions
trans-unit.target: Descriptions

trans-unit.source: 1. Minimal
trans-unit.target: 1. Minimal

trans-unit.source: Households are able to meet essential food and non-food needs without engaging in atypical and unsustainable strategies to access food and income.
trans-unit.target: Households are able to meet essential food and non-food needs without engaging in atypical and unsustainable strategies to access food and income.

trans-unit.source: 2. Stressed
trans-unit.target: 2. Stressed

trans-unit.source: Households have minimally adequate food consumption but are unable to afford some essential non-food expenditures without engaging in stress-coping strategies.
trans-unit.target: Households have minimally adequate food consumption but are unable to afford some essential non-food expenditures without engaging in stress-coping strategies.

trans-unit.source: 3. Crisis
trans-unit.target: 3. Crisis

trans-unit.source: Households either have food consumption gaps that are reflected by high or above-usual acute malnutrition __OR__ are marginally able to meet minimum food needs but only by depleting essential livelihood assets or through crisis-coping strategies.
trans-unit.target: Households either have food consumption gaps that are reflected by high or above-usual acute malnutrition __OR__ are marginally able to meet minimum food needs but only by depleting essential livelihood assets or through crisis-coping strategies.

trans-unit.source: 4. Emergency
trans-unit.target: 4. Emergency

trans-unit.source: Households either have large food consumption gaps which are reflected in very high acute malnutrition and excess mortality; __OR__ are able to mitigate large food consumption gaps but only by employing emergency livelihood strategies and asset liquidation.
trans-unit.target: Households either have large food consumption gaps which are reflected in very high acute malnutrition and excess mortality; __OR__ are able to mitigate large food consumption gaps but only by employing emergency livelihood strategies and asset liquidation.

trans-unit.source: 5. Famine
trans-unit.target: 5. Famine

trans-unit.source: Households have an extreme lack of food and/or other basic needs even after full employment of coping strategies. Starvation, death, destitution, and extremely critical acute malnutrition levels are evident. (For Famine Classification, area needs to have extreme critical levels of acute malnutrition and mortality.)
trans-unit.target: Households have an extreme lack of food and/or other basic needs even after full employment of coping strategies. Starvation, death, destitution, and extremely critical acute malnutrition levels are evident. (For Famine Classification, area needs to have extreme critical levels of acute malnutrition and mortality.)

trans-unit.source: IPC Food Security Projection:
trans-unit.target: IPC Food Security Projection:

trans-unit.source: Three times a year (February, June, and October) FEWSNET estimates most likely IPC classes for the upcoming 8 month (near-term and mid-term projection), available from 2019-current. The near-term projection is called ML1 and is a projection for the upcoming 4 month, the mid-term projection is called ML2 and projects the IPC classes for the 4 subsequent months. For the triggering ML1 (near-term) as well as ML2 (mid-term) projections will be considered.
trans-unit.target: Three times a year (February, June, and October) FEWSNET estimates most likely IPC classes for the upcoming 8 month (near-term and mid-term projection), available from 2019-current. The near-term projection is called ML1 and is a projection for the upcoming 4 month, the mid-term projection is called ML2 and projects the IPC classes for the 4 subsequent months. For the triggering ML1 (near-term) as well as ML2 (mid-term) projections will be considered.

trans-unit.source: :::{admonition} UPDATE: IPC Classification Data :type: attention
trans-unit.target: :::{admonition} UPDATE: IPC Classification Data :type: attention

trans-unit.source: The food security classification projections are generally published twice a year and usually includes a projection for a period of three months and a current phase, which also spans three months. Due to the unavailability of FEWSNET projections, the trigger model is using the [IPC data](www.ipcinfo.org/ipc-country-analysis/details-map/en/c/1156097/?iso3=SOM)
trans-unit.target: The food security classification projections are generally published twice a year and usually includes a projection for a period of three months and a current phase, which also spans three months. Due to the unavailability of FEWSNET projections, the trigger model is using the [IPC data](www.ipcinfo.org/ipc-country-analysis/details-map/en/c/1156097/?iso3=SOM)

trans-unit.source: Outlook updates are produced almost every month and are also taken into account.
trans-unit.target: Outlook updates are produced almost every month and are also taken into account.

trans-unit.source: IPC-Population Weighted Index
trans-unit.target: IPC-Population Weighted Index

trans-unit.source: To better operationalise the IPC data a simple population-weighted index was developed. Relative population numbers are weighted based on the respective IPC class they falling, in order to give the amount of people in a certain IPC class the importance instead of the IPC class only. Furthermore, population located in a higher IPC class is more important than population located in a lower class. The index is calculated as follows:
trans-unit.target: To better operationalise the IPC data a simple population-weighted index was developed. Relative population numbers are weighted based on the respective IPC class they falling, in order to give the amount of people in a certain IPC class the importance instead of the IPC class only. Furthermore, population located in a higher IPC class is more important than population located in a lower class. The index is calculated as follows:

trans-unit.source: $ IPC\ Index = Weights \times \frac{District\ Pop\ per\ IPC\ Phase}{Total\ District\ Pop}$
trans-unit.target: $ IPC\ Index = Weights \times \frac{District\ Pop\ per\ IPC\ Phase}{Total\ District\ Pop}$

trans-unit.source: Where the weights are defined as:
trans-unit.target: Where the weights are defined as:

trans-unit.target: IPC Phase

trans-unit.target: Weight

trans-unit.target: IPC 1

trans-unit.target: IPC 2

trans-unit.target: IPC 3

trans-unit.target: IPC 4

trans-unit.source: IPC 5
trans-unit.target: IPC 5

trans-unit.source: The IPC Index represents low-population districts equal to high-population districts. No under-representation of high food insecurity of small districts occurs.
trans-unit.target: The IPC Index represents low-population districts equal to high-population districts. No under-representation of high food insecurity of small districts occurs.

trans-unit.source: Trigger Workflow Automated
trans-unit.target: Trigger Workflow Automated

trans-unit.source: As explained in the beginning of this [chapter](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/GIS_AA/en_qgis_drought_trigger_somalia.html#qgis-trigger-workflow-for-somalia), the 9 main steps of the developed trigger workflow are done automatically by a QGIS model. In the previous chapters you have learned the purpose and needed tools of each step and how to perform them manually. In this chapter it is explained how to run the automated model.
trans-unit.target: As explained in the beginning of this [chapter](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/GIS_AA/en_qgis_drought_trigger_somalia.html#qgis-trigger-workflow-for-somalia), the 9 main steps of the developed trigger workflow are done automatically by a QGIS model. In the previous chapters you have learned the purpose and needed tools of each step and how to perform them manually. In this chapter it is explained how to run the automated model.

trans-unit.source: The [QGIS Model Designer](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_automatisation_wiki.html#the-qgis-model-designer) is a visual tool that allows users to create and edit a workflow with all tools available in QGIS that can be used repeatedly in a simple and time-efficient manner. It provides a graphical interface to build workflows by connecting geoprocessing tools and algorithms. The user can define inputs, outputs, and the flow of data between different processing steps.
trans-unit.target: The [QGIS Model Designer](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_automatisation_wiki.html#the-qgis-model-designer) is a visual tool that allows users to create and edit a workflow with all tools available in QGIS that can be used repeatedly in a simple and time-efficient manner. It provides a graphical interface to build workflows by connecting geoprocessing tools and algorithms. The user can define inputs, outputs, and the flow of data between different processing steps.

trans-unit.source: :::{admonition} Troubleshooting the model :class: note
trans-unit.target: :::{admonition} Troubleshooting the model :class: note

trans-unit.source: When updating the monitoring, make sure you compare the new input layers with the previous input layers. It can happen that the data structure from FEWSNET or ICPAC changes. For example, the attribute columns are called differently, or the values for the classes change. In these cases, the model can break and output error messages.
trans-unit.target: When updating the monitoring, make sure you compare the new input layers with the previous input layers. It can happen that the data structure from FEWSNET or ICPAC changes. For example, the attribute columns are called differently, or the values for the classes change. In these cases, the model can break and output error messages.

trans-unit.source: To fix these issues, take a look at the subsection on [Troubleshooting the model](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/GIS_AA/en_qgis_drought_trigger_somalia.html#troubleshooting-the-model).
trans-unit.target: To fix these issues, take a look at the subsection on [Troubleshooting the model](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/GIS_AA/en_qgis_drought_trigger_somalia.html#troubleshooting-the-model).

trans-unit.source: Step 1: Setting up folder structure
trans-unit.target: Step 1: Setting up folder structure

trans-unit.source: __Purpose:__ In this step we set up the correct folder structure to make the analysis easier and to ensure consistent results.
trans-unit.target: __Purpose:__ In this step we set up the correct folder structure to make the analysis easier and to ensure consistent results.

trans-unit.source: __Tool:__ No special tools or programs are needed
trans-unit.target: __Tool:__ No special tools or programs are needed

trans-unit.source: Step 2: Download of the forecast data
trans-unit.target: Step 2: Download of the forecast data

trans-unit.source: __Tool:__ FileZilla and Internet Browser
trans-unit.target: __Tool:__ FileZilla and Internet Browser

trans-unit.source: The current plans provide that ICPAC will monthly provide the SPI-12 forecast whereas the IPC data will be pulled from the FEWSNET website. FEWS NET publishes IPC data on its website. The main data publications plus the updates of the IPC data amount to the publication of new data almost monthly.
trans-unit.target: The current plans provide that ICPAC will monthly provide the SPI-12 forecast whereas the IPC data will be pulled from the FEWSNET website. FEWS NET publishes IPC data on its website. The main data publications plus the updates of the IPC data amount to the publication of new data almost monthly.

trans-unit.source: SPI-12 Data
trans-unit.target: SPI-12 Data

trans-unit.source: ICPAC will provide the SPI-12 forecasts on their FTP (File Transfer Protocol). There are different ways to access the FTP-Server. We recommend to install and once set up [FileZilla](https://filezilla-project.org/download.php?platform=win64). This will make your work easily repeatable on a monthly basis.
trans-unit.target: ICPAC will provide the SPI-12 forecasts on their FTP (File Transfer Protocol). There are different ways to access the FTP-Server. We recommend to install and once set up [FileZilla](https://filezilla-project.org/download.php?platform=win64). This will make your work easily repeatable on a monthly basis.

trans-unit.source: Download Filezilla [here](https://filezilla-project.org/download.php?platform=win64).
trans-unit.target: Download Filezilla [here](https://filezilla-project.org/download.php?platform=win64).

trans-unit.source: Install the `.exe` file you have downloaded
trans-unit.target: Install the `.exe` file you have downloaded

trans-unit.source: Open FileZilla
trans-unit.target: Open FileZilla

trans-unit.source: Establish a connection to the FTP Server by inserting the credentials you have been passed (Host, Username and Password) and clicking `Quickconnect`.
trans-unit.target: Establish a connection to the FTP Server by inserting the credentials you have been passed (Host, Username and Password) and clicking `Quickconnect`.

trans-unit.source: In FileZilla you have four windows. On the left hand side you will see the folder on your computer in the upper window. By clicking on a folder, the documents in the folder will be shown in the lower left window. On the right hand side, you will see in the upper window the FTP data folder and by clicking on it, the data will be shown in the lower right window.
trans-unit.target: In FileZilla you have four windows. On the left hand side you will see the folder on your computer in the upper window. By clicking on a folder, the documents in the folder will be shown in the lower left window. On the right hand side, you will see in the upper window the FTP data folder and by clicking on it, the data will be shown in the lower right window.

trans-unit.source: In order to pass the data from the FTP Server to your own machine you can simply drag and drop the folder or data from the right hand side windows (FTP-Server) to the left hand side windows (your Computer). To do so, firstly navigate to your folder where you need the latest SPI-12 data to be located `.../FbF_Drought_Monitoring_Trigger/Monitoring/Year_Month_template/SPI_12`. Then drag and drop the latest SPI-12 into the folder.
trans-unit.target: In order to pass the data from the FTP Server to your own machine you can simply drag and drop the folder or data from the right hand side windows (FTP-Server) to the left hand side windows (your Computer). To do so, firstly navigate to your folder where you need the latest SPI-12 data to be located `.../FbF_Drought_Monitoring_Trigger/Monitoring/Year_Month_template/SPI_12`. Then drag and drop the latest SPI-12 into the folder.

trans-unit.source: IPC Data
trans-unit.target: IPC Data

trans-unit.source: The IPC Projection data is provided and regularly updated on the [IPC Website](https://www.ipcinfo.org/ipc-country-analysis/details-map/en/c/1159510/?iso3=SOM). To navigate to the latest IPC Projection data on Somalia, navigate to `Latest Analyses` in the top bar > `IPC Analyses` > `Acute Food Insecurity Classification`. Here you look for the latest analysis for Somalia.
trans-unit.target: The IPC Projection data is provided and regularly updated on the [IPC Website](https://www.ipcinfo.org/ipc-country-analysis/details-map/en/c/1159510/?iso3=SOM). To navigate to the latest IPC Projection data on Somalia, navigate to `Latest Analyses` in the top bar > `IPC Analyses` > `Acute Food Insecurity Classification`. Here you look for the latest analysis for Somalia.

trans-unit.source: Go to the [IPC website](https://www.ipcinfo.org)
trans-unit.target: Go to the [IPC website](https://www.ipcinfo.org)

trans-unit.source: In the top bar, navigate to `Latest Analyses` > `IPC Analyses` > `Acute Food Insecurity Classification`.
trans-unit.target: In the top bar, navigate to `Latest Analyses` > `IPC Analyses` > `Acute Food Insecurity Classification`.

trans-unit.source: On the new website, select Somalia as a country and select the newest dataset.
trans-unit.target: On the new website, select Somalia as a country and select the newest dataset.

trans-unit.source: On this website, you will see both a map of the current and the projected IPC phase classifications, as well as some metadata. On the right side of the map, click on the button `Download GIS format`. This will download the analysis in a GeoJSON format, containing polygons for the administrative boundaries and IPC phases, as well as points for the IPC phase classification for IDP camps.
trans-unit.target: On this website, you will see both a map of the current and the projected IPC phase classifications, as well as some metadata. On the right side of the map, click on the button `Download GIS format`. This will download the analysis in a GeoJSON format, containing polygons for the administrative boundaries and IPC phases, as well as points for the IPC phase classification for IDP camps.

trans-unit.source: Download the __GeoJSON file__. The filename is composed of the country name, the analysis type, and Year and month of publication. E.g., `Somalia-Acute Food Insecurity January 2025.geoJSON`
trans-unit.target: Download the __GeoJSON file__. The filename is composed of the country name, the analysis type, and Year and month of publication. E.g., `Somalia-Acute Food Insecurity January 2025.geoJSON`

trans-unit.source: In some cases, your operating system (Windows) misidentifies the GeoJSON-file as a `.customization`-file. This does not change anything and can be loaded into your QGIS-project.
trans-unit.target: In some cases, your operating system (Windows) misidentifies the GeoJSON-file as a `.customization`-file. This does not change anything and can be loaded into your QGIS-project.

trans-unit.source: Copy the GeoJSON-file into the input monitoring folder.
trans-unit.target: Copy the GeoJSON-file into the input monitoring folder.

trans-unit.source: Example path: `/FbF_Drought_Monitoring_Trigger/Input_monitoring/Year_Month_template/Somalia-Acute Food Insecurity January 2025.geoJSON`
trans-unit.target: Example path: `/FbF_Drought_Monitoring_Trigger/Input_monitoring/Year_Month_template/Somalia-Acute Food Insecurity January 2025.geoJSON`

trans-unit.source: Step 3: Loading data into QGIS
trans-unit.target: Step 3: Loading data into QGIS

trans-unit.source: __Purpose:__ In this step, all the data needed will be loaded into QGIS.
trans-unit.target: __Purpose:__ In this step, all the data needed will be loaded into QGIS.

trans-unit.source: __Tool:__ No specific tools are needed, only QGIS.
trans-unit.target: __Tool:__ No specific tools are needed, only QGIS.

trans-unit.source: Open QGIS and create a [new project](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projects_folder_structure_wiki.html#step-by-step-setting-up-a-new-qgis-project-from-scratch) by clicking on `Project` -> `New`

trans-unit.source: Once the project is created save the project in the folder you created in Step 1 (e.g. 2022_05). To do that click on `Project` -> `Save as` and navigate to the folder. Give the project the same name as the folder you created (e.g. 2022_05). Then click `Save`

You can find the template in the following directory: ".../FbF_Drought_Monitoring_Trigger/maps_somalia_template_risk_assessment.qpt".
trans-unit.target: In order to easily visualize the output of the trigger analysis we provide you with a [map template](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Module_4/en_qgis_map_design_2.html#map-templates) that can be used as a base for your visualization. You can find the template in the following directory: ".../FbF_Drought_Monitoring_Trigger/maps_somalia_template_risk_assessment.qpt".

trans-unit.source: You can also adapt the template to your needs and preferences. You can find help [here](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Module_4/en_qgis_map_design_2.html#print-layout).

trans-unit.source: Troubleshooting the Model
trans-unit.target: Troubleshooting the Model

trans-unit.source: In some cases, for example when the data structure of the input layers change, the model can break. To make sure that the model functions correctly, follow these troubleshooting steps. In most cases, the problems are happening at the input stage (yellow boxes).
trans-unit.target: In some cases, for example when the data structure of the input layers change, the model can break. To make sure that the model functions correctly, follow these troubleshooting steps. In most cases, the problems are happening at the input stage (yellow boxes).

trans-unit.source: When monitoring the trigger, take a closer look at the new data downloaded from FEWSNET or ICPAC. Are there any changes in the names of the columns or values?
trans-unit.target: When monitoring the trigger, take a closer look at the new data downloaded from FEWSNET or ICPAC. Are there any changes in the names of the columns or values?

trans-unit.source: If you get an error message, read the error message. Which input or which step is producing the error?
trans-unit.target: If you get an error message, read the error message. Which input or which step is producing the error?

trans-unit.source: Take a look at the attribute table of the input layer that is responsible for this layer and at the processing algorithm (white boxes) this layer is feeding into. You can open the parameters for the algorithms by clicking on the `...` in the algorithm box. A new window will open with the parameters for the algorithm. What value is the algorithm expecting? What values or data is available in the input layer?
trans-unit.target: Take a look at the attribute table of the input layer that is responsible for this layer and at the processing algorithm (white boxes) this layer is feeding into. You can open the parameters for the algorithms by clicking on the `...` in the algorithm box. A new window will open with the parameters for the algorithm. What value is the algorithm expecting? What values or data is available in the input layer?

trans-unit.source: Adjust the values the algorithms is expecting or preprocess the input layer so the columns and values are exactly what the algorithm expects.
trans-unit.target: Adjust the values the algorithms is expecting or preprocess the input layer so the columns and values are exactly what the algorithm expects.

trans-unit.source: __For example__,
trans-unit.target: __For example__,

trans-unit.source: FEWSNET changed the structure of the IPC Food Security Projection Data:
trans-unit.target: FEWSNET changed the structure of the IPC Food Security Projection Data:

trans-unit.source: The data for ML1 and ML2 is no longer available as two distinct shapefiles, but the (overlapping) polygons for both scenarios are contained in a single geojson file.
trans-unit.target: The data for ML1 and ML2 is no longer available as two distinct shapefiles, but the (overlapping) polygons for both scenarios are contained in a single geojson file.

trans-unit.source: Furthermore, the values for the [different food insecurity phase](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/GIS_AA/en_qgis_drought_trigger_somalia.html#what-is-ipc-food-security-projection-data) (1 to 5: Minimal, Stressed, Crisis, Emergency, Famine) are no longer in a column called "ML1" and "ML2" respectively, but in a column with the name __"value"__.
trans-unit.target: Furthermore, the values for the [different food insecurity phase](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/GIS_AA/en_qgis_drought_trigger_somalia.html#what-is-ipc-food-security-projection-data) (1 to 5: Minimal, Stressed, Crisis, Emergency, Famine) are no longer in a column called "ML1" and "ML2" respectively, but in a column with the name __"value"__.

trans-unit.source: The model components "weighing pop-sum based on IPC phase" are expecting a column with the name "ML1" and "ML2", but the columns with this information (IPC Phase) with the new input layers are no longer called "ML1" and "ML2" and are called "value".
trans-unit.target: The model components "weighing pop-sum based on IPC phase" are expecting a column with the name "ML1" and "ML2", but the columns with this information (IPC Phase) with the new input layers are no longer called "ML1" and "ML2" and are called "value".

trans-unit.source: To fix the model, we need to do the following:
trans-unit.target: To fix the model, we need to do the following:

trans-unit.source: Extract the "ML1" and "ML2" polygons as two separate vector layers. We can use the tool "Extract by Attribute".
trans-unit.target: Extract the "ML1" and "ML2" polygons as two separate vector layers. We can use the tool "Extract by Attribute".

trans-unit.source: In the field calculator, we need to adjust the expression so it includes the correct column. Change "ML1" and "ML2" into "value" respectively.
trans-unit.target: In the field calculator, we need to adjust the expression so it includes the correct column. Change "ML1" and "ML2" into "value" respectively.

trans-unit.source: Trigger Workflow Manually
trans-unit.target: Trigger Workflow Manually

trans-unit.source: As of may 2025, the trigger model uses the IPC Phase Classification analysis by the IPC and no longer by FEWSNET. The data format has changed and the manual workflow no longer works as described below. The updated workflow will be added soon. For now, please use the automated workflow above.
trans-unit.target: As of may 2025, the trigger model uses the IPC Phase Classification analysis by the IPC and no longer by FEWSNET. The data format has changed and the manual workflow no longer works as described below. The updated workflow will be added soon. For now, please use the automated workflow above.

trans-unit.source: Step 1: Setting up folder structure
trans-unit.target: Step 1: Setting up folder structure

trans-unit.source: __Purpose:__ In this step we set up the correct folder structure to make the analysis easier and to ensure consitent results.
trans-unit.target: __Purpose:__ In this step we set up the correct folder structure to make the analysis easier and to ensure consitent results.

trans-unit.source: __Tool:__ No special tools or programs are needed.
trans-unit.target: __Tool:__ No special tools or programs are needed.

trans-unit.source: The Video below shows the process for setting up the folder for decmber 2023.
trans-unit.target: The Video below shows the process for setting up the folder for decmber 2023.

trans-unit.source: Step 2: Download of the forecast data
trans-unit.target: Step 2: Download of the forecast data

trans-unit.source: __Tool:__ FileZilla and Interent Browser
trans-unit.target: __Tool:__ FileZilla and Interent Browser

trans-unit.source: The current plans provide that ICPAC will monthly provide the SPI-12 forcast whereas the IPC data will be pulled from the FEWSNET website. FEWS NET publishes IPC data on its website. The main data publications plus the updates of the IPC data amount to the publication of new data almost monthly.
trans-unit.target: The current plans provide that ICPAC will monthly provide the SPI-12 forcast whereas the IPC data will be pulled from the FEWSNET website. FEWS NET publishes IPC data on its website. The main data publications plus the updates of the IPC data amount to the publication of new data almost monthly.

trans-unit.source: SPI-12 Data
trans-unit.target: SPI-12 Data

trans-unit.source: ICPAC will provide the SPI-12 forecasts on their FTP (File Transfer Protocol). There are different ways to access the FTP-Server. We recommend to install and once set up [FileZilla](https://filezilla-project.org/download.php?platform=win64). This will make your work easily repeatable on a monthly basis.
trans-unit.target: ICPAC will provide the SPI-12 forecasts on their FTP (File Transfer Protocol). There are different ways to access the FTP-Server. We recommend to install and once set up [FileZilla](https://filezilla-project.org/download.php?platform=win64). This will make your work easily repeatable on a monthly basis.

trans-unit.source: Download Filezilla [here](https://filezilla-project.org/download.php?platform=win64).
trans-unit.target: Download Filezilla [here](https://filezilla-project.org/download.php?platform=win64).

trans-unit.source: Install the `.exe` file you have downloaded.
trans-unit.target: Install the `.exe` file you have downloaded.

trans-unit.source: Open FileZilla.
trans-unit.target: Open FileZilla.

trans-unit.source: Establish a connection to the FTP Server by insterting the credentials you have been passed (Host, Username and Password) and clicking `Quickconnect`.
trans-unit.target: Establish a connection to the FTP Server by insterting the credentials you have been passed (Host, Username and Password) and clicking `Quickconnect`.

trans-unit.source: In FileZilla you have four windows. On the left hand side you will see the folder on your computer in the upper window. By clicking on a folder, the documents in the folder will be shown in the lower left window. On the right hand side, you will see in the upper window the FTP data folder and by clicking on it, the data will be shown in the lower right window.
trans-unit.target: In FileZilla you have four windows. On the left hand side you will see the folder on your computer in the upper window. By clicking on a folder, the documents in the folder will be shown in the lower left window. On the right hand side, you will see in the upper window the FTP data folder and by clicking on it, the data will be shown in the lower right window.

trans-unit.source: In order to pass the data from the FTP Server to your own machine you can simply drag and drop the folder or data from the righthandside windows (FTP-Server) to the lefthandside windows (your Computer). To do so, firstly navigate to your folder where you wneed the latest SPI-12 data to be located `.../FbF_Drought_Monitoring_Trigger/Monitoring/Year_Month_template/SPI_12`. Then drag and drop the latest SPI-12 into the folder.
trans-unit.target: In order to pass the data from the FTP Server to your own machine you can simply drag and drop the folder or data from the righthandside windows (FTP-Server) to the lefthandside windows (your Computer). To do so, firstly navigate to your folder where you wneed the latest SPI-12 data to be located `.../FbF_Drought_Monitoring_Trigger/Monitoring/Year_Month_template/SPI_12`. Then drag and drop the latest SPI-12 into the folder.

trans-unit.source: IPC Data
trans-unit.target: IPC Data

trans-unit.source: IPC Data
trans-unit.target: IPC Data

trans-unit.source: The IPC Projection data is provided and regularly updated on the [IPC Website](https://www.ipcinfo.org/ipc-country-analysis/details-map/en/c/1159510/?iso3=SOM). To navigate to the latest IPC Projection data on Somalia, navigate to `Latest Analyses` in the top bar > `IPC Analyses` > `Acute Food Insecurity Classification`. Here you look for the latest analysis for Somalia.
trans-unit.target: The IPC Projection data is provided and regularly updated on the [IPC Website](https://www.ipcinfo.org/ipc-country-analysis/details-map/en/c/1159510/?iso3=SOM). To navigate to the latest IPC Projection data on Somalia, navigate to `Latest Analyses` in the top bar > `IPC Analyses` > `Acute Food Insecurity Classification`. Here you look for the latest analysis for Somalia.

trans-unit.source: Go to the [IPC website](https://www.ipcinfo.org)
trans-unit.target: Go to the [IPC website](https://www.ipcinfo.org)

trans-unit.source: In the top bar, navigate to `Latest Analyses` > `IPC Analyses` > `Acute Food Insecurity Classification`.
trans-unit.target: In the top bar, navigate to `Latest Analyses` > `IPC Analyses` > `Acute Food Insecurity Classification`.

trans-unit.source: On the new website, select Somalia as a country and select the newest dataset.
trans-unit.target: On the new website, select Somalia as a country and select the newest dataset.

trans-unit.source: On this website, you will see both a map of the current and the projected IPC phase classifications, as well as some metadata. On the right side of the map, click on the button `Download GIS format`. This will download the analysis in a GeoJSON format, containing polygons for the administrative boundaries and IPC phases, as well as points for the IPC phase classification for IDP camps.
trans-unit.target: On this website, you will see both a map of the current and the projected IPC phase classifications, as well as some metadata. On the right side of the map, click on the button `Download GIS format`. This will download the analysis in a GeoJSON format, containing polygons for the administrative boundaries and IPC phases, as well as points for the IPC phase classification for IDP camps.

trans-unit.source: Download the __GeoJSON file__. The filename is composed of the country name, the analysis type, and Year and month of publication. E.g., `Somalia-Acute Food Insecurity January 2025.geoJSON`
trans-unit.target: Download the __GeoJSON file__. The filename is composed of the country name, the analysis type, and Year and month of publication. E.g., `Somalia-Acute Food Insecurity January 2025.geoJSON`

trans-unit.source: In some cases, your operating system (Windows) misidentifies the GeoJSON-file as a `.customization`-file. This does not change anything and can be loaded into your QGIS-project.
trans-unit.target: In some cases, your operating system (Windows) misidentifies the GeoJSON-file as a `.customization`-file. This does not change anything and can be loaded into your QGIS-project.

trans-unit.source: Copy the GeoJSON-file into the input monitoring folder.
trans-unit.target: Copy the GeoJSON-file into the input monitoring folder.

trans-unit.source: Example path: `/FbF_Drought_Monitoring_Trigger/Input_monitoring/Year_Month_template/Somalia-Acute Food Insecurity January 2025.geoJSON`
trans-unit.target: Example path: `/FbF_Drought_Monitoring_Trigger/Input_monitoring/Year_Month_template/Somalia-Acute Food Insecurity January 2025.geoJSON`

trans-unit.source: Step 3: Loading data into QGIS
trans-unit.target: Step 3: Loading data into QGIS

trans-unit.source: __Purpose:__ In this step, all the data needed will be loaded into QGIS.
trans-unit.target: __Purpose:__ In this step, all the data needed will be loaded into QGIS.

trans-unit.source: __Tool:__ No specific tools are needed, only QGIS.
trans-unit.target: __Tool:__ No specific tools are needed, only QGIS.

trans-unit.source: Open QGIS and create a [new project](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projects_folder_structure_wiki.html#step-by-step-setting-up-a-new-qgis-project-from-scratch) by clicking on `Project` -> `New`
trans-unit.target: Open QGIS and create a [new project](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projects_folder_structure_wiki.html#step-by-step-setting-up-a-new-qgis-project-from-scratch) by clicking on `Project` -> `New`

trans-unit.source: Once the project is created save the project in the folder you created in Step 1 (e.g. 2022_05). To do that click on `Project` -> `Save as` and navigate to the folder. Give the project the same name as the folder you created (e.g. 2022_05). Then click `Save`
trans-unit.target: Once the project is created save the project in the folder you created in Step 1 (e.g. 2022_05). To do that click on `Project` -> `Save as` and navigate to the folder. Give the project the same name as the folder you created (e.g. 2022_05). Then click `Save`

trans-unit.source: Load all input data in QGIS by [drag and drop](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.html#open-raster-data-via-drag-and-drop). Click on `Project` -> `Save`
trans-unit.target: Load all input data in QGIS by [drag and drop](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.html#open-raster-data-via-drag-and-drop). Click on `Project` -> `Save`

trans-unit.source: From the folder you created in step 1
trans-unit.target: From the folder you created in step 1

trans-unit.source: ML1 and ML2 (the GeoJSON-format contains both ML1 and ML2 in one layer)
trans-unit.target: ML1 and ML2 (the GeoJSON-format contains both ML1 and ML2 in one layer)

trans-unit.source: SPI-12
trans-unit.target: SPI-12

trans-unit.source: From the `Fixed_data` folder:
trans-unit.target: From the `Fixed_data` folder:

trans-unit.source: district_pop_som
trans-unit.target: district_pop_som

trans-unit.source: Regions
trans-unit.target: Regions

trans-unit.source: risk_assessment.gpkg
trans-unit.target: risk_assessment.gpkg

trans-unit.source: WorldPop_som.tif
trans-unit.target: WorldPop_som.tif

trans-unit.source: __Result:__ QGIS project with all necessary data ready to be analysed.
trans-unit.target: __Result:__ QGIS project with all necessary data ready to be analysed.

trans-unit.source: This video shows how to setup the QGIS project for April 2022 and how to import all data into the project.
trans-unit.target: This video shows how to setup the QGIS project for April 2022 and how to import all data into the project.

trans-unit.source: Step 4: Extracting ML1 and ML2 from the GeoJSON layer
trans-unit.target: Step 4: Extracting ML1 and ML2 from the GeoJSON layer

trans-unit.source: __Purpose:__ The GeoJSON file from FEWSNET with the IPC data contains both the ML1 and ML2 polygons in the same layer. In order to process these polygons separately in the next step, we need to extract and save them as new layers.
trans-unit.target: __Purpose:__ The GeoJSON file from FEWSNET with the IPC data contains both the ML1 and ML2 polygons in the same layer. In order to process these polygons separately in the next step, we need to extract and save them as new layers.

trans-unit.source: __Tool:__ "Extract by Attributes"
trans-unit.target: __Tool:__ "Extract by Attributes"

trans-unit.source: Step 5: Fixing the Geometries for the ML 1 & ML 2 layers
trans-unit.target: Step 5: Fixing the Geometries for the ML 1 & ML 2 layers

trans-unit.source: __Purpose:__ The aim is to fix the [errors in the geometries](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Module_3/en_qgis_digitalisation.html#spatial-digitisation-errors-in-qgis) of each layer. Otherwise, we would receive error messages in the further steps.
trans-unit.target: __Purpose:__ The aim is to fix the [errors in the geometries](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Module_3/en_qgis_digitalisation.html#spatial-digitisation-errors-in-qgis) of each layer. Otherwise, we would receive error messages in the further steps.

trans-unit.source: __Tool:__ Fix geometries
trans-unit.target: __Tool:__ Fix geometries

trans-unit.source: Step 6: Intersection of ML 1 & ML 2 data with the district polygons
trans-unit.target: Step 6: Intersection of ML 1 & ML 2 data with the district polygons

trans-unit.source: __Purpose:__ The goal is to receive polygon layers which share both the borders and the attributes of both input layers.
trans-unit.target: __Purpose:__ The goal is to receive polygon layers which share both the borders and the attributes of both input layers.

trans-unit.source: __Tool:__ [`Intersection`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_geoprocessing_wiki.html#intersection)
trans-unit.target: __Tool:__ [`Intersection`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_geoprocessing_wiki.html#intersection)

trans-unit.source: __Result:__ After doing this for ML1 and ML2 you should have two polygon layers, each containing all columns of ML1 (or ML2) and district_pop_sum.
trans-unit.target: __Result:__ After doing this for ML1 and ML2 you should have two polygon layers, each containing all columns of ML1 (or ML2) and district_pop_sum.

trans-unit.source: The video shows the whole process on the example of ML 1.
trans-unit.target: The video shows the whole process on the example of ML 1.

trans-unit.source: Step 7: Calculation of Population per Intersection Polygon
trans-unit.target: Step 7: Calculation of Population per Intersection Polygon

trans-unit.source: __Purpose:__ Here we calculate the population in each polygon of the intersection layer from step 4.
trans-unit.target: __Purpose:__ Here we calculate the population in each polygon of the intersection layer from step 4.

trans-unit.source: __Tool:__ [`Zonal Statistics`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_raster_basic_wiki.html#zonal-statistics)
trans-unit.target: __Tool:__ [`Zonal Statistics`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_raster_basic_wiki.html#zonal-statistics)

trans-unit.source: __Result:__ The result should be the “ML1_zonal_statistic” and “ML2_zonal_statistic” polygon layers. These layers should have the same columns in the attribute table __plus__ the column “_sum”, which is the number of people living in the single parts of the polygons.
trans-unit.target: __Result:__ The result should be the “ML1_zonal_statistic” and “ML2_zonal_statistic” polygon layers. These layers should have the same columns in the attribute table __plus__ the column “_sum”, which is the number of people living in the single parts of the polygons.

trans-unit.source: Step 8: Weighting of the Population based on IPC-Phase
trans-unit.target: Step 8: Weighting of the Population based on IPC-Phase

trans-unit.source: __Purpose:__ The purpose of this step is the weighting of the population in the five IPC phases as described in [IPC Data](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/GIS_AA/en_qgis_drought_trigger_somalia.html#ipc-population-weighted-index).
trans-unit.target: __Purpose:__ The purpose of this step is the weighting of the population in the five IPC phases as described in [IPC Data](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/GIS_AA/en_qgis_drought_trigger_somalia.html#ipc-population-weighted-index).

trans-unit.source: __Tool:__ [`Field Calculator`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_table_functions_wiki.html#calculate-field)
trans-unit.target: __Tool:__ [`Field Calculator`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_table_functions_wiki.html#calculate-field)

trans-unit.source: Right-click on the layer “ML1_zonal_statistic” (or “ML2_zonal_statistic”) -> `Open Attribute Table`-> click on [`Field Calculator`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_table_functions_wiki.html#calculate-field) ![](/fig/mActionCalculateField.png) to open the field calculator
trans-unit.target: Right-click on the layer “ML1_zonal_statistic” (or “ML2_zonal_statistic”) -> `Open Attribute Table`-> click on [`Field Calculator`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_table_functions_wiki.html#calculate-field) ![](/fig/mActionCalculateField.png) to open the field calculator

trans-unit.source: Check “Create new field"
trans-unit.target: Check “Create new field"

trans-unit.source: `Output field name`: Name the new column “pop_sum_weighted”
trans-unit.target: `Output field name`: Name the new column “pop_sum_weighted”

trans-unit.source: `Result field type`: Decimal number (real)
trans-unit.target: `Result field type`: Decimal number (real)

trans-unit.source: Add the code block from Input into the `Expression` field and click `ok`
trans-unit.target: Add the code block from Input into the `Expression` field and click `ok`

trans-unit.source: Save the new column by clicking on ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) in the attribute table and end the editing mode by clicking on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png)
trans-unit.target: Save the new column by clicking on ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) in the attribute table and end the editing mode by clicking on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png)

trans-unit.source: Here is the `Field Calculator` window of how it should look to calculate pop_sum_weighted for ML1.
trans-unit.target: Here is the `Field Calculator` window of how it should look to calculate pop_sum_weighted for ML1.

trans-unit.source: __Result:__ The two layers “ML1_zonal_statistic” and “ML2_zonal_statistic” should now both have the column “pop_sum_weighted”.
trans-unit.target: __Result:__ The two layers “ML1_zonal_statistic” and “ML2_zonal_statistic” should now both have the column “pop_sum_weighted”.

trans-unit.source: The video shows the whole process with the the example of ML 1.
trans-unit.target: The video shows the whole process with the the example of ML 1.

trans-unit.source: Step 9: Adding the total district population to Intersection Polygons
trans-unit.target: Step 9: Adding the total district population to Intersection Polygons

trans-unit.source: __Purpose:__ Now we want to add a column with the total district population to “ML1_zonal_statistic” and “ML2_zonal_statistic”.
trans-unit.target: __Purpose:__ Now we want to add a column with the total district population to “ML1_zonal_statistic” and “ML2_zonal_statistic”.

trans-unit.source: __Tool:__ [`Join attributes by field value`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_non_spatial_joins_wiki.html#join-attributes-by-field-value)
trans-unit.target: __Tool:__ [`Join attributes by field value`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_non_spatial_joins_wiki.html#join-attributes-by-field-value)

trans-unit.source: __Result:__ Now you should have to new polygon layer named “ML1_join” and ML2_Join” containing the column districtpo.
trans-unit.target: __Result:__ Now you should have to new polygon layer named “ML1_join” and ML2_Join” containing the column districtpo.

trans-unit.source: Step 10: Calculation of the Population Proportion per Intersection Polygon
trans-unit.target: Step 10: Calculation of the Population Proportion per Intersection Polygon

trans-unit.source: __Purpose:__ In this step we calculating the [IPC-Population Weighted Index](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/GIS_AA/en_qgis_drought_trigger_somalia.html#ipc-population-weighted-index) for every small part of the polygon layer.
trans-unit.target: __Purpose:__ In this step we calculating the [IPC-Population Weighted Index](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/GIS_AA/en_qgis_drought_trigger_somalia.html#ipc-population-weighted-index) for every small part of the polygon layer.

trans-unit.source: __Tool:__[`Field Calculator`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_table_functions_wiki.html#calculate-field)
trans-unit.target: __Tool:__[`Field Calculator`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_table_functions_wiki.html#calculate-field)

trans-unit.source: Right-click on Intersection_population Polygons layer -> “Attribute Table”-> click on [`Field Calculator`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_table_functions_wiki.html#calculate-field) ![](/fig/mActionCalculateField.png)to open the field calculator
trans-unit.target: Right-click on Intersection_population Polygons layer -> “Attribute Table”-> click on [`Field Calculator`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_table_functions_wiki.html#calculate-field) ![](/fig/mActionCalculateField.png)to open the field calculator

trans-unit.source: Check `Create new field`
trans-unit.target: Check `Create new field`

trans-unit.source: `Output field name`: Name the new column “Index_per_IPCPolygon_ML1” (or "Index_per_IPCPolygon_ML2”)
trans-unit.target: `Output field name`: Name the new column “Index_per_IPCPolygon_ML1” (or "Index_per_IPCPolygon_ML2”)

trans-unit.source: `Result field type`: Decimal number (real)
trans-unit.target: `Result field type`: Decimal number (real)

trans-unit.source: Add the code into the `Expression` field
trans-unit.target: Add the code into the `Expression` field

trans-unit.source: Click `ok`
trans-unit.target: Click `ok`

trans-unit.source: Save the new column by clicking on ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) in the attribute table and end the editing mode by clicking on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png)
trans-unit.target: Save the new column by clicking on ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) in the attribute table and end the editing mode by clicking on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png)

trans-unit.source: __Result:__ Both layer “ML1_join” and ML2_Join” should now have the column “Index_per_IPCPolygon_ML1” or “Index_per_IPCPolygon_ML2”. The numbers in this column have to be smaller than in the “district” column.
trans-unit.target: __Result:__ Both layer “ML1_join” and ML2_Join” should now have the column “Index_per_IPCPolygon_ML1” or “Index_per_IPCPolygon_ML2”. The numbers in this column have to be smaller than in the “district” column.

trans-unit.source: The video shows the whole process with the the example of ML 1.
trans-unit.target: The video shows the whole process with the the example of ML 1.

trans-unit.source: Step 11: Calculate the IPC Index per District
trans-unit.target: Step 11: Calculate the IPC Index per District

trans-unit.source: __Purpose:__ The purpose of this step is to calculate a population weighted mean over the IPC classes that fall within a district, in order to give the amount of people living in a certain IPC class more importance than just the area affected by a certain IPC class. The result is a IPC Index value for each district.
trans-unit.target: __Purpose:__ The purpose of this step is to calculate a population weighted mean over the IPC classes that fall within a district, in order to give the amount of people living in a certain IPC class more importance than just the area affected by a certain IPC class. The result is a IPC Index value for each district.

trans-unit.source: __Tool:__ `Join attribute by location (summary)`
trans-unit.target: __Tool:__ `Join attribute by location (summary)`

trans-unit.source: __Result:__ As a result, your two layers "ML1_join_location" and "ML2_join_location" should have the column “Index_per_IPCPolygon_ML1_mean” or “Index_per_IPCPolygon_ML2_mean”. Furthermore, the number of rows should be the exact number of districts in Somalia and the polygons should have the exact shape of the districts.
trans-unit.target: __Result:__ As a result, your two layers "ML1_join_location" and "ML2_join_location" should have the column “Index_per_IPCPolygon_ML1_mean” or “Index_per_IPCPolygon_ML2_mean”. Furthermore, the number of rows should be the exact number of districts in Somalia and the polygons should have the exact shape of the districts.

trans-unit.source: Step 12.: Join ML1 and ML2
trans-unit.target: Step 12.: Join ML1 and ML2

trans-unit.source: __Purpose:__ The purpose of this step is to merge “ML1_join_location" and "ML2_join_location” into one layer so we have the IPC-Index for all districts.
trans-unit.target: __Purpose:__ The purpose of this step is to merge “ML1_join_location" and "ML2_join_location” into one layer so we have the IPC-Index for all districts.

trans-unit.source: __Tool:__ [`Join attributes by field value`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_non_spatial_joins_wiki.html#join-attributes-by-field-value)
trans-unit.target: __Tool:__ [`Join attributes by field value`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_non_spatial_joins_wiki.html#join-attributes-by-field-value)

trans-unit.source: __Result:__ Layer with the districts of Somalia and the IPC-Index of each district.
trans-unit.target: __Result:__ Layer with the districts of Somalia and the IPC-Index of each district.

trans-unit.source: Step 13: Calculation of SPI-12 Mean per District
trans-unit.target: Step 13: Calculation of SPI-12 Mean per District

trans-unit.source: __Purpose:__ Calculate the mean value over the SPI-12 values of all pixels that fall within a scertain districts area, in order to have one SPI-12 value for each district.
trans-unit.target: __Purpose:__ Calculate the mean value over the SPI-12 values of all pixels that fall within a scertain districts area, in order to have one SPI-12 value for each district.

trans-unit.source: __Tool:__ [`Zonal Statistics`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_raster_basic_wiki.html#zonal-statistics)
trans-unit.target: __Tool:__ [`Zonal Statistics`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_raster_basic_wiki.html#zonal-statistics)

trans-unit.source: __Result:__ A layer of all districts of Somalia with the mean SPI-12.
trans-unit.target: __Result:__ A layer of all districts of Somalia with the mean SPI-12.

trans-unit.source: Step 14: Join SPI-12 Mean to the IPC Index
trans-unit.target: Step 14: Join SPI-12 Mean to the IPC Index

trans-unit.source: __Purpose:__ The purpose of this step is to merge data from two different data sources into one data frame so that it can be jointly analysed.
trans-unit.target: __Purpose:__ The purpose of this step is to merge data from two different data sources into one data frame so that it can be jointly analysed.

trans-unit.source: __Tool:__[`Join attributes by field value`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_non_spatial_joins_wiki.html#join-attributes-by-field-value)
trans-unit.target: __Tool:__[`Join attributes by field value`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_non_spatial_joins_wiki.html#join-attributes-by-field-value)

trans-unit.source: __Result:__ The result will be a layer of all districts of Somalia with the mean SPI-12 and the IPC-Index of each district.
trans-unit.target: __Result:__ The result will be a layer of all districts of Somalia with the mean SPI-12 and the IPC-Index of each district.

trans-unit.source: Step 15: Evaluate the Trigger Activation
trans-unit.target: Step 15: Evaluate the Trigger Activation

trans-unit.source: __Purpose:__ The purpose of this step is to gain a quick overview of possible trigger activation without having to revise the actual data. Instead we will have a binary column with trigger = yes or trigger=no values.
trans-unit.target: __Purpose:__ The purpose of this step is to gain a quick overview of possible trigger activation without having to revise the actual data. Instead we will have a binary column with trigger = yes or trigger=no values.

trans-unit.source: __Tool:__ [`Field Calculator`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_table_functions_wiki.html#calculate-field)
trans-unit.target: __Tool:__ [`Field Calculator`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_table_functions_wiki.html#calculate-field)

trans-unit.source: Right-click on "IPC_index_SPI_12_district" layer -> `Attribute Table`-> click on [`Field Calculator`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_table_functions_wiki.html#calculate-field) ![](/fig/mActionCalculateField.png) to open the field calculator
trans-unit.target: Right-click on "IPC_index_SPI_12_district" layer -> `Attribute Table`-> click on [`Field Calculator`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_table_functions_wiki.html#calculate-field) ![](/fig/mActionCalculateField.png) to open the field calculator

trans-unit.source: Check `Create new field`
trans-unit.target: Check `Create new field`

trans-unit.source: `Output field name`: Name the new column “Trigger_activation”
trans-unit.target: `Output field name`: Name the new column “Trigger_activation”

trans-unit.source: `Result field type`: Text (string)
trans-unit.target: `Result field type`: Text (string)

trans-unit.source: Add the code below into the `Expression` field
trans-unit.target: Add the code below into the `Expression` field

trans-unit.source: Click `ok`
trans-unit.target: Click `ok`

trans-unit.source: Save the new column by clicking on ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) in the attribute table and end the editing mode by clicking on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png)
trans-unit.target: Save the new column by clicking on ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) in the attribute table and end the editing mode by clicking on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png)

trans-unit.source: __Result:__ A layer with all districts of Somalia with a column of "Yes" and "No" values indicating whether the trigger levels have been reached or not.
trans-unit.target: __Result:__ A layer with all districts of Somalia with a column of "Yes" and "No" values indicating whether the trigger levels have been reached or not.

trans-unit.target: __Purpose:__ Viualization of the map features in a printable map layout

trans-unit.source: __Tool:__ [Print Layout](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Module_4/en_qgis_map_design_2.html?highlight=print+layout#print-layout)
trans-unit.target: __Tool:__ [Print Layout](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Module_4/en_qgis_map_design_2.html?highlight=print+layout#print-layout)

trans-unit.source: If not done before, delet all layers expect __Trigger_activation__, __risk_assessment_districts__ and __OpenStreetMap__
trans-unit.target: If not done before, delet all layers expect __Trigger_activation__, __risk_assessment_districts__ and __OpenStreetMap__

trans-unit.source: Open a new print layout by clicking on `Project` -> `New Print Layout` -> enter the name of your current Project e.g "2022_04".
trans-unit.target: Open a new print layout by clicking on `Project` -> `New Print Layout` -> enter the name of your current Project e.g "2022_04".

trans-unit.source: Go the the __FbF_Drought_Monitoring_Trigger__` folder and drag and drop the file `Trigger_activation_Intervention_map.qpt` in the print layout
trans-unit.target: Go the the __FbF_Drought_Monitoring_Trigger__` folder and drag and drop the file `Trigger_activation_Intervention_map.qpt` in the print layout

trans-unit.source: Change the date to the current date by clicking on "Further map info…" in the items panel. Click on the `Item Properties` tab and scroll down. Here you can change the date in the `Main Properties` field.
trans-unit.target: Change the date to the current date by clicking on "Further map info…" in the items panel. Click on the `Item Properties` tab and scroll down. Here you can change the date in the `Main Properties` field.

trans-unit.source: Adjust the Lgend by clicking on the legend in the `Item Properties` tab and scroll down until you see the `Legend items` field. If it is not there check if you have to open the dropdown. Make sure `Auto update` is not checked.
trans-unit.target: Adjust the Lgend by clicking on the legend in the `Item Properties` tab and scroll down until you see the `Legend items` field. If it is not there check if you have to open the dropdown. Make sure `Auto update` is not checked.

trans-unit.source: Remove all itemes in the legend be clicking on the item and then on the red minus icon below.
trans-unit.target: Remove all itemes in the legend be clicking on the item and then on the red minus icon below.

trans-unit.source: Add __Trigger_activation__ and __risk_assessment_districts__ to the legend by clicking on the green plus and click on the layer and click `ok`
trans-unit.target: Add __Trigger_activation__ and __risk_assessment_districts__ to the legend by clicking on the green plus and click on the layer and click `ok`

trans-unit.source: In order to easily visualize the output of the trigger analysis we provide you with a [map template](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Module_4/en_qgis_map_design_2.html#map-templates) that can be used as a base for your visualization. You can find the template in the following directory: ".../FbF_Drought_Monitoring_Trigger/maps_somalia_template_risk_assessment.qpt".
trans-unit.target: In order to easily visualize the output of the trigger analysis we provide you with a [map template](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Module_4/en_qgis_map_design_2.html#map-templates) that can be used as a base for your visualization. You can find the template in the following directory: ".../FbF_Drought_Monitoring_Trigger/maps_somalia_template_risk_assessment.qpt".

trans-unit.source: You can also adapt the template to your needs and preferences. You can find help [here](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Module_4/en_qgis_map_design_2.html#print-layout).
trans-unit.target: You can also adapt the template to your needs and preferences. You can find help [here](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Module_4/en_qgis_map_design_2.html#print-layout).

trans-unit.source: Step 18: Exporting the Map
trans-unit.target: Step 18: Exporting the Map

trans-unit.source: __Purpose:__ Export the designed and finalized map layout in order tp print it as a pdf or format of your choice.
trans-unit.target: __Purpose:__ Export the designed and finalized map layout in order tp print it as a pdf or format of your choice.

trans-unit.source: __Tool:__ [Print Layout](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Module_4/en_qgis_map_design_2.html?highlight=print+layout#print-layout)
trans-unit.target: __Tool:__ [Print Layout](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Module_4/en_qgis_map_design_2.html?highlight=print+layout#print-layout)

trans-unit.target: In the print layout click on `Layer` -> `Export as Image`
